title: Dubai Location Approvals: What Producers Should Confirm First
alt: Dubai city locations prepared for a commercial film shoot
excerpt: Before locking a UAE shoot schedule, confirm location ownership, authority requirements, access windows, and any brand or drone restrictions.
content:
Location approval is often the first practical checkpoint for a UAE production.

A producer should confirm whether the site is public, private, managed by a destination authority, or connected to a landmark with separate rules.

Access timing, crew size, vehicles, lighting, drone activity, and brand visibility can all affect the approval path.

Checking these details early helps prevent late schedule changes and gives the permit application a cleaner route through review.

title: Drone Filming in the UAE: Planning Before You Fly
alt: Drone filming preparation for a UAE production
excerpt: Drone shoots need careful planning around location, flight scope, operator requirements, safety, timing, and supporting approvals.
content:
Drone filming in the UAE should be planned as a regulated production activity, not as a last-minute add-on.

The approval path can depend on the emirate, exact location, flight area, operator credentials, shoot purpose, and whether the content is commercial.

Production teams should prepare the flight brief, intended angles, dates, nearby landmarks, and any ground support requirements before submission.

Early coordination gives the team a better chance of aligning creative needs with compliant flight planning.

title: Equipment Customs for UAE Film Shoots: A Simple Prep Guide
alt: Professional camera equipment packed for customs clearance
excerpt: A smoother entry starts with accurate equipment lists, serial numbers, shipment details, and a realistic customs timeline.
content:
International crews bringing film equipment into the UAE should prepare documentation before travel or shipment.

Typical planning includes a detailed equipment list, serial numbers, values, ownership information, arrival details, and the intended production schedule.

Missing or inconsistent details can slow clearance and create pressure on the first shoot day.

Coordinating equipment paperwork alongside permit planning helps keep the production schedule realistic from the start.

title: Do You Really Need a Filming Permit in Dubai? (What Most People Get Wrong in 2026)
alt: Film crew working near the Dubai skyline
excerpt: A practical guide to when permits are required for commercial shoots, branded content, documentaries, and creator productions.
content:
If you are planning a commercial shoot in Dubai, a filming permit is not optional, it is a requirement.

This applies to advertising campaigns, branded content, corporate productions, and social media advertising.

Dubai operates under a structured regulatory framework, and any commercial filming activity must be approved through the relevant authorities.

title: Filming Permits in Dubai: Who Can Apply?
alt: Dubai skyline at sunset with a film camera operator
excerpt: Understand the usual documents, sponsor requirements, and project details needed before a UAE filming application can move forward.
content:
Dubai is home to visually striking filming locations, but professional production is regulated.

Applicants usually need to prepare project details, production dates, locations, crew information, equipment lists, and intended usage before an application can move forward.

Working through a local production support partner helps clarify which approvals are needed and how the permit should be submitted.

title: Production Logistics Checklist for UAE Shoots
alt: Cinema equipment cases moving through airport handling
excerpt: From gear customs to transport, crew calls, hotels, and vendor coordination, this checklist helps international teams prepare.
content:
A smooth UAE shoot starts before the crew lands.

Confirm permit requirements, customs documents, accommodation, transportation, equipment rental, location access, crew call times, and vendor support early.

A local production support partner can coordinate the operational details so the production team can focus on the creative brief.
